title: Long Document Example
subtitle: Long-form editorial chapter, report, or essay.

sections.heading: Long Document in use
sections.body: This long-form document demonstrates a chapter rhythm with sections, tables, diagrams, and source policy notes.

cards.title: Chapter 1
cards.body: Why portable HTML artifacts help review workflows.

cards.title: Chapter 2
cards.body: How schemas, templates, and static checks keep output predictable.

cards.title: Chapter 3
cards.body: How examples should teach the template catalog.

sections.heading: Portable artifacts
sections.body: A portable artifact should be useful after it leaves the app. It needs readable hierarchy, local assets, visible source notes, and enough embedded interaction to demonstrate the idea without a server.

sections.heading: Template evidence
sections.body: A template proves itself by showing the data it expects. Reports should include status data, diagrams should include diagrams, and editor demos should include editable JSON.

sections.heading: Checklist
items: Does the page answer what the template is for?
Does it include representative visual structure?
Can it be understood in light mode and print?
